decider_instructions: Close ISS-1668, ISS-1669, ISS-1670 as duplicates of ISS-1613, ISS-1614, ISS-1615 respectively (already fixed by EXP-268, integrated commit cf661aa→d06d75d on 2026-04-27T22:05:10Z). duplication_finding.summary: EXP-274 is a duplicate of EXP-268. ISS-1668/1669/1670 are duplicates of ISS-1613/1614/1615. All three holes were already addressed by EXP-268's three additive footnotes, integrated 2026-04-27T22:05:10Z (workspace-sync commit d06d75d). The current §4.2.7a HTML (length 14,209 chars vs cf661aa's 11,531) contains every recommended fix from the EXP-260 c1 review's holes_found list.
duplication_finding.likely_root_cause: Decider re-scanned curmudgeon review monitor/curmudgeon/reviews/EXP-260-proposal.c1.json and re-created issues from its holes_found list without checking whether the review was already digested or whether matching closed issues already point at it. The c1 review's queue_id=270 is recorded as popped_by_queue_id=270 at 2026-04-27T18:22:12Z (the same run that originally created ISS-1613/1614/1615), which suggests the dedup signal is present but was not consulted on this round.

verification.method: Compared current HEAD section (d06d75d and later workspace-syncs) against the curmudgeon's specific recommendation text in EXP-260-proposal.c1.json. Confirmed all three additive paragraphs landed and contain the curmudgeon's recommended numbers verbatim or to the cited rounding.

H2_AE_projection_convention.curmudgeon_required: Footnote acknowledging convention choice; show kill survives the alternative (Ebro 222 km, Canary 2,042 km, closest-pair Δr 75 km, max 1 → max 2, Moon altitude ~123,000 km, ~49×).
H2_AE_projection_convention.current_text_status: ADDRESSED — paragraph 'Robustness check (projection convention)' contains: 'Ebro becomes 222 km from the projected path, Canary 2,042 km, and the closest-pair Δr between Coimbra and San Pablo halves to 75 km — just inside the 85-km band, which would shift the strict constant-r reading from "max 1 station in band" to "max 2." … The Moon altitude required to inflate R_p to even the closest min-distance under that convention is about 123,000 km — still ~49× the dome's published 2,534 km.' All curmudgeon-recommended numbers present.

H3_NN_tour_to_area_coverage.curmudgeon_required: Either (a) replace 16,439 km NN claim with area-coverage argument already downstream, or (b) compute a stricter NN lower bound. Drop the unsourced 442-km band-width number.
H3_NN_tour_to_area_coverage.current_text_status: ADDRESSED via BOTH options. The brittle '16,439 km > 15,660 km' claim is replaced with a hedged formulation: 'a tour visiting all nine of the dome's published stations spans at least 12,000 km on the disc from any starting point — the shortest greedy nearest-neighbour route runs about 12,400 km, longer routes run to 16,400 km' (acknowledges start-point dependence, gives lower bound). Then leans on area coverage: '85-km-wide band along that arc covers ... roughly 0.1% of the 1.26 billion km² disc surface' and explicit chance-coincidence framing: 'the probability that all nine fall within the ~0.1% area swept by an 85-km band along a 3-hour arc is on the order of 10^-27 — vanishingly small.' The unsourced 442-km band-width number is gone.

H4_observed_vs_physical_altitude.curmudgeon_required: One sentence noting dome publishes h_Sun ≤ 4,200 km physical alongside 5,733 km observed; show kill strengthens under physical reading (R_p drops to ~31 km).
H4_observed_vs_physical_altitude.current_text_status: ADDRESSED — paragraph 'Robustness check (altitude convention)' contains: 'The dome's eclipse page also publishes a separate physical Sun altitude — h_Sun ≤ 4,200 km (firmament limit) — alongside the 5,733 km observed altitude, with the implication that the observed value is a refraction-bent reading of the smaller physical altitude. Under that alternative, scaling h_Moon proportionally to ~1,860 km, the penumbra ground radius drops to roughly 31 km, the band narrows to ~62 km wide, and every per-station distance becomes a larger multiple of R_p. The kill is robust to the altitude convention; under the dome's own "physical" reading it strengthens.' All curmudgeon-recommended numbers present.

anticipated_objections.objection: Maybe the decider re-flagged these because something REGRESSED in §4.2.7a between EXP-268's integration and now (e.g., a later patch or workspace-sync overwrote the additive paragraphs).
anticipated_objections.response: Verified by direct text inspection of HEAD data/sections.json: all three EXP-268 paragraphs are present, in the exact form recommended by the curmudgeon. The 14,209-char section length (vs cf661aa's 11,531) accounts for the ~2,700 chars EXP-268 added; nothing has been removed since. No regression.

anticipated_objections.objection: Maybe the curmudgeon issued a c2 or later cycle that re-raised the same holes with new severity.
anticipated_objections.response: No c2/c3 review file for EXP-260 exists in monitor/curmudgeon/reviews/. The only EXP-260 review is c1, the same one whose holes were already fixed via ISS-1613/1614/1615.

anticipated_objections.objection: Maybe the SVG H0 is still missing and the issues were re-raised because the integration is still incomplete.
anticipated_objections.response: ISS-1650 (the H0/SVG critical) is closed. EXP-273 (svg-asset-resolution) was the dedicated fix and is complete. SVG file docs/assets/eclipse-stations-vs-penumbra-band.svg can be confirmed present in repo via ls; the section's tag now resolves.

kernel_of_truth: The holes the curmudgeon flagged were genuine and substantive — the original EXP-260 was structurally sound but had three real defensive openings (projection convention, NN-tour fragility, altitude defense). EXP-268 closed all three cleanly with additive paragraphs that converted defender openings into demonstrations of robustness. The decider re-flagging them today is a process bug, not a content judgement; the underlying review work is and remains correct.
why_kernel_doesnt_save_claim: N/A — this is a process-level no-op, not a substantive scientific claim.
other_side_of_the_argument: The strongest case AGAINST closing as duplicate would be that the H2 paragraph is technically a 'Robustness check' rather than a footnote (the curmudgeon's recommended form), so a strict reading might say it doesn't fully match the recommended structure. But the substance — acknowledging the convention choice and showing the kill survives with specific numbers — is exactly what the curmudgeon asked for, and an inline robustness paragraph is structurally stronger than a footnote (it's harder for a defender to dismiss). The form difference is editorial, not substantive. Recommendation stands: close as duplicate.
